Financial Performance

Step-by-step savings calculation from historical benchmark to earned savings/losses.

Step Description Value MSSP Median Peer Median
1 Assigned Beneficiaries 17,800 p63 13,159 18,574
2 Historical Benchmark (per capita) $14,647 p94 $11,439 $11,834
3 Regional Adjustment +$235
4 Prior Savings Adjustment +-
5 Adjustment Category Regional Adjustment
6 Updated Benchmark (per capita) $17,485 p94 $13,279 $13,538
7 Total Benchmark (all beneficiaries) $305,072,639
8 Total Expenditure (all beneficiaries) $295,006,937
9 Per Capita Expenditure (PY) $16,908 p95 $12,693 $12,501
10 Generated Savings / Losses $10,065,702 $6,931,306 —
11 Savings Rate 3.30% p39 4.21% 5.13%
12 Minimum Savings Rate (threshold) 2.59%
13 Guardrail Applied —
14 Final Shared Savings Rate 50.00%
15 Final Shared Loss Rate 30.00%
16 Earned Savings / Losses $4,932,194 $3,685,876 —
Revenue-to-Expense Category High Revenue

Annual per-beneficiary expenditure across benchmark years (BY1–BY3) and the performance year (PY). Includes Parts A and B FFS spending.

Population Segment BY1BY2BY3PY Trend Risk-Adj. Expected Difference

Aged, Non-Dual $11,758 $12,415 $12,970 $15,742 ↑ $12,336 +$3,406
Total $16,908 $12,693 $12,501

Risk Scores

Hierarchical Condition Category risk scores. A score of 1.0 = national average. Below 1.0 means the population is healthier than average.

CMS-HCC Risk Scores

Population Segment BY1BY2BY3PY Trend Coding Gap MSSP Med.
End-Stage Renal Disease (ESRD) 1.088 1.040 1.018 1.038 ↓ +0.037 0.967
Disabled 1.112 1.071 1.085 1.001 ↓ +0.027 0.934
Aged, Dual-Eligible 1.150 1.071 1.067 1.047 ↓ +0.037 0.954
Aged, Non-Dual 1.053 1.055 1.055 1.023 → -0.010 0.983

Demographic Risk Scores

Demographic-only risk scores (age/sex/Medicaid status). Compared with HCC risk scores, the difference reflects clinical coding intensity.

Population Segment BY3PY
End-Stage Renal Disease (ESRD) — 1.001
Disabled — 0.974
Aged, Dual-Eligible — 1.010
Aged, Non-Dual — 1.033

Risk Redistribution Weights (PY)

Fraction of the ACO's total risk attributable to each population segment.

ESRD Weight 4.6%
Disabled Weight 7.5%
Aged Dual Weight 7.8%
Aged Non-Dual Weight 80.1%

Clinical Quality Measures

MSSP quality measures. Measures marked "lower is better" are inverted — a lower score indicates better performance.

Measure This ACO MSSP Median Peer Median
Overall Quality Score (Composite) p83 89.86 83.13 83.71
All-Cause Unplanned Admissions (risk-adjusted rate) (lower is better) ? p53 0.15 0.15 0.16

Peer Cohort

30 similar mid-size, ENHANCED-track, average acuity, hospital-affiliated ACOs

View 30 peer ACOs
A4825A4592A1059A5357A5370A4776A1241A3016A3899A2710A3236A3336A3047A2490A5124A4556A4654A4551A5372A5088A5040A3630A4711A1026A5193A2207A4894A2097A5419A5364

Peers selected using weighted distance across 15 structural features (population acuity, composition, scale, care infrastructure, assignment type) within the ENHANCED track partition. Learn more

Methodology

Data source: MSSP ACO Performance PY2024 (dataset ID: mssp-performance). Vintage: PY2024. Downloaded: 2026-04-12. Rows in source: 476.

MSSP ACO Performance reports financial and quality performance for Accountable Care Organizations participating in the Medicare Shared Savings Program. Savings/losses are calculated against risk-adjusted, regionally-weighted benchmarks.

Full methodology →

MIT License. Free. No ads. No login.
GitHub · Report an error
